content: In Zod v4, `.default({})` on a nested object schema does NOT cascade inner field defaults — the `{}` is used literally, so inner fields remain undefined. Use `.default(() => Schema.parse({}))` (function form) so each parse gets a fresh defaulted object. The static form `.default(Schema.parse({}))` shares array/object references across parses, causing mutation leaks between consumers.
context: Discovered during Phase 1A (core types + config) — 2026-02-18 handover. Still load-bearing: src/config/types.ts relies on the function-form default for ReviewStandardsSchema, PlanReviewStandardsSchema, CodeReviewStandardsSchema, PrecommitStandardsSchema. Any future nested-default addition must follow the same pattern. Regression test lives in src/config/loader.test.ts around the mutation-isolation case.
title: Zod v4: nested defaults don't cascade
